questions.question: Explain polynomial regression and why it is still considered a linear model.
keyPoints: Uses polynomial features: X, X², X³...
Non-linear in features but linear in parameters
Coefficients are still estimated using OLS
Feature transformation converts non-linear problem to linear

questions.question: Discuss the relationship between polynomial degree and model complexity, including the risk of overfitting.
keyPoints: Higher degree = more parameters = more complexity
Low degree may underfit (miss non-linear patterns)
High degree overfits (captures noise, wiggly curves)

questions.question: How is polynomial regression implemented using linear regression techniques?
keyPoints: Create new features: X², X³, up to desired degree
Feature scaling is important for numerical stability

questions.question: Compare polynomial regression with linear regression. When would you choose one over the other?
keyPoints: Linear: simple, interpretable, for linear relationships
Use residual plots to check if linear is adequate
